title: [Feature]: Add payload encryption key rotation and secret-manager patterns
problem: Core payload encryption supports AES-based encryption before sink writes, but long-running deployments need documented key rotation, key identifiers, and secret-manager integration patterns. Without guidance and helper support, operators may keep one key too long or store key material in unsafe locations.
proposal: Add key rotation guidance and, where appropriate, helper support for multi-key decryption based on key identifiers in encrypted payload envelopes. Document integration patterns for platform secret stores without adding hard dependencies to every runtime installation. Keep direct key material out of public examples and prefer environment-backed or secret-manager-backed runtime injection.
users: Security teams, operators running encrypted payload storage, and maintainers extending encryption behavior for future sinks.
delivery_semantics: Key rotation must not change ACK ordering. Encryption must complete before sink delivery, and decryption helpers must be offline or operator tooling unless a future trusted transformation sink is explicitly designed. Failed encryption setup must fail before ACK.
security: Keys must remain separate from encrypted data, must not appear in logs or issue comments, and should support versioned key identifiers. Rotation examples must avoid real secret-store paths or live environment details.
acceptance: Documentation explains rotation, key identifiers, and multi-key decryption concepts.
Helper APIs are added only if they preserve current encrypted envelope compatibility.
Secret-manager integration remains optional and does not add unnecessary runtime dependencies.
Tests cover old-key and new-key decryption behavior if helper code is added.
tests: Add unit tests for any key registry or multi-key decryption helper. Run scripts/check-encryption.sh and scripts/check.sh. If documentation-only, run markdown and security scans.
documentation: Update docs/payload-encryption.md, docs/security.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/operations.md, ROBOTS.md, AGENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after release publication. Include release tag, rotation model, tests run, docs updated, and explicit limitations around secret-store implementations.
